raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command to count differing lines between two files. Use diff -y? Or diff -u? To count lines that differ: diff -q? But count lines. Use diff -y | grep -c -e '|'? Actually diff -y shows side by side with | for differences. Count lines containing |. So: diff -y /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | grep -c '|' . That outputs count. Alternatively use diff -u and wc -l? But diff -u shows context. Better: diff -y --suppress-common-lines | wc -l. That counts differing lines. So command: diff -y --suppress-common-lines /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | wc -l.

That outputs count. Alternatively use diff -u and wc -l? But diff -u shows context. Better: diff -y --suppress-common-lines | wc -l. That counts differing lines. So command: diff -y --suppress-common-lines /workspace/dir1/long.txt /workspace/dir1/terminate.txt | wc -l.
